author: fab_10
content:
Creating a thread so we can deep dive in this, since I need some more clarification.

if I understand correctly, the CL can send an initial FcU+IL and then update it with FcU+IL(2) for the same payload creation. 
In this case what happens in the EL, is implementation dependent, for example in Besu, at the moment, the previous block creation is cancelled, and a new one starts, also because the new FcU+IL(2) will generate a different payloadIdentifier.

author: fab_10
content:
Besu block creation is an iterative process, it continues to try to build new blocks for the same FcU, until the getPayload is called, that's because fresh arrived txs can be included.

To respond to your question about the timing, for Besu the process will take the time to create a block with only the IL txs as first attempt
